rule_name: Context budget management for ingest process
description: Principles and mechanisms for managing what stays in agent context during ingest. Based on context engineering best practices (Anthropic, LangChain). Context is a finite budget — every token competes for attention.

principles.context_as_budget: The context window is a finite resource with diminishing marginal returns. Every new token depletes the attention budget by some amount. Do not hold more than necessary at any point.
principles.tool_result_clearing_principle: Once tool results (file contents, search outputs) are processed and their extracted knowledge written to disk — the raw content becomes re-fetchable noise. Clear it from context while keeping metadata that points to where it's stored.
principles.just_conclusion_rule: If intermediate output (source docs, generated wiki pages) is disposable and only its conclusion matters — discard it after extraction/integration. The wiki IS the memory; conversation context should contain operations, not data dumps.
principles.context_boundary_markers: Use explicit boundaries between phases: <read_phase> → <extract_write_phase> → <clear_and_proceed>. Model follows these as signal to forget content in previous phase.

lifecycle_phases.action: extract entities/concepts/facts → write to wiki page

lifecycle_phases.trigger: after EXTRACT_WRITE completes and page is written to disk

raw_source_documents.contract: Full raw content must NOT persist beyond the turn where it was processed. If agent needs to re-read, grep wiki page (which has captured the knowledge) — not the original source.

lifecycle_phases.trigger: after validation + crosslinks verified and written to disk

generated_wiki_pages_during_ingest.rationale: Wiki pages are disk-persisted. Once created, validated, and linked — they are retrievable via grep/read. Holding their full content in context during subsequent steps is token waste.
generated_wiki_pages_during_ingest.exception: If another step NEEDS to reference this page's content (e.g., reading existing page for update), read it fresh from disk rather than holding previous content.

context_bubble_policy.rule: When opening a wiki page to read — close (forget) one of the current 3. This is the 'context bubble' policy.

forget_mechanism_for_agent_instructions.description: How to explicitly instruct the agent to forget/release content from context
forget_mechanism_for_agent_instructions.pattern: After completing extraction/writing of a source, add explicit instruction: 'Source document content is now stored in wiki page [path]. Discard full source text from working context. Continue with next step using only metadata.'

xml_boundary_style.description: Use XML-like markers to delineate phases where forget should happen
xml_boundary_style.rationale: Models trained on XML structures parse namespaces and boundaries reliably. These markers serve as explicit signal.

explicit_forget_phrases: Discard full source text — knowledge captured in wiki page at [path]
Source is now transient — only path metadata remains
Page content written + validated → discard from context; retrievable via read_file if needed later

after_each_source_read.instruction: After step_3_analysis completes (source read and entities extracted), agent must explicitly forget the source content. The knowledge is now in the wiki page being created/updated.
after_each_source_read.marker_pattern: step_3_complete → [FORGET SOURCE CONTENT] → proceed to template selection

after_wiki_page_write_and_validate.instruction: After step_9 post_checks complete (page written, structure validated, crosslinks created), agent must forget the full page content. Page is retrievable via wiki path.
after_wiki_page_write_and_validate.marker_pattern: step_9_complete → [FORGET PAGE CONTENT] → proceed to registration and logging

comparison_with_research_best_practices.anthropic_context_engineering_alignment: This rule implements the 'tool-result clearing' primitive: once re-fetchable data is processed, drop it from context while keeping metadata. Also applies 'sub-agent delegation' principle: each source is processed in isolation and its content discarded.
comparison_with_research_best_practices.langchain_context_editing_alignment: Mirrors LangChain's ContextEditingMiddleware approach — surgically removing stale tool results (file reads) after they've served their purpose, replacing them with placeholders (knowledge captured in wiki).

five_turn_decisions_mapping.continue: Not applicable — ingest has clear task boundaries
five_turn_decisions_mapping.rewind: If validation fails → reset to step_8a/8b and retry
five_turn_decisions_mapping.clear: Between sources: clear previous source content before reading next
five_turn_decisions_mapping.compact: Not used during ingest (compaction is for long conversations)
five_turn_decisions_mapping.delegate: Each source processed independently — implicit delegation pattern
